---
title: "Step 2 -- Zonal statistics (optional)"
format: html
---

<!-- For detailed instructions, see:
     https://worldbank.github.io/devPTIpack/articles/build-pti-2-zonal-stats.html -->

::: {.callout-note}
**This step is optional.** Use it when you need to extract zonal
statistics from raster data (e.g. satellite imagery, gridded
population) before building your metadata Excel in Step 3. It is
**not** sourced by `00-master.R` -- run it manually as needed.

This file ships as a stub; populate the chunks below with your own
extraction code, write the resulting indicator columns into a CSV /
Excel keyed by `admin<N>Pcod`, and feed that file into Step 3.

Add `02b-...qmd`, `02c-...qmd`, etc. for additional zonal-stats
extractions.
:::

## When you need zonal stats

Typical use cases:

- Population-weighted averages of poverty rasters by district.
- Mean night-time-lights intensity per province.
- Average accessibility / travel-time per polygon.

## Common tools

- [`exactextractr`](https://isciences.gitlab.io/exactextractr/) -- fast
  zonal stats with fractional pixel coverage.
- [`terra::extract()`](https://rspatial.github.io/terra/reference/extract.html)
  -- general-purpose raster sampling.

## Output format

One column per indicator, keyed by `admin<N>Pcod`. The output of this
step becomes one of the indicator-value sheets you load into your
metadata Excel in Step 3 (see [Data preparation reference](
https://worldbank.github.io/devPTIpack/articles/dataprep.html)
for the column contract).

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Example skeleton -- replace with your own raster + admin layer.
# library(sf)
# library(terra)
# library(exactextractr)
#
# adm2 <- sf::read_sf("sample-data/rwa_adm2.geojson")
# r    <- terra::rast("path/to/your-raster.tif")
# adm2$mean_value <- exactextractr::exact_extract(r, adm2, fun = "mean")
#
# # Write a tidy table for Step 3.
# adm2 |>
#   sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
#   dplyr::transmute(admin2Pcod = shapeID, my_indicator = mean_value) |>
#   writexl::write_xlsx("sample-data/my-zonal-stats-adm2.xlsx")
```
